name: Rajasthan
stopoverNote: The Jaisalmer→Udaipur leg is a 9-hour haul — break it at Jodhpur or Ranakpur, or take a train/flight, rather than driving it in one day.

diet.vegetarian: Rajasthan is one of India's most vegetarian-friendly states. Thalis, dal baati churma, gatte ki sabzi and street snacks (pyaaz kachori, mirchi vada) are almost all pure vegetarian; many towns like Pushkar are strictly veg and alcohol-free.
diet.vegan: Ask for food 'bina ghee, doodh, dahi' (no ghee, milk or curd). Ker sangri, bajra roti with garlic chutney and oil-cooked dal are naturally vegan. Millets of Mewar (Udaipur) has a strong dedicated vegan menu.
phrases: Sirf tel mein banao = cook only in oil, no ghee

cities.name: Jaipur
cities.notes: The Pink City — Amber Fort, City Palace, Hawa Mahal and buzzing bazaars, all UNESCO-listed old town.

cities.name: Pushkar
cities.notes: Compact holy lakeside town with the rare Brahma Temple, ghats and boho cafés; vegetarian and alcohol-free by custom.

cities.name: Jodhpur
cities.notes: The Blue City below the mighty Mehrangarh Fort — indigo lanes, stepwells and a lively clock-tower bazaar.

cities.name: Jaisalmer
cities.notes: The Golden City — a living sandstone fort, ornate havelis and the Thar desert dunes at Sam.

cities.name: Udaipur
cities.notes: The City of Lakes — Lake Pichola, the sprawling City Palace, garden retreats and hilltop sunset views.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM (Jio or Airtel) works well; buy one at the airport or a store with photo ID. Coverage is strong across cities but patchy in the deep desert around Sam.

Jaipur.name: Jaipur
Jaipur.note: Two days is ideal — one for Amber Fort and the old-city monuments, one for forts, bazaars and culture.

days.note: The old-city monuments are clustered and walkable — start Amber by 8am to beat the heat and crowds; four adjacent stops is the realistic maximum for one day.

activities.name: Amber (Amer) Fort — Sheesh Mahal & ramparts
activities.tip: Walk up or take a jeep; skip the elephant rides on welfare grounds.

activities.name: Panna Meena ka Kund stepwell + Jal Mahal photo stop
Hawa Mahal façade
activities.tip: The best view is from a Wind View Café terrace across the street.

activities.name: City Palace museum & courtyards
activities.tip: Not part of the composite ticket — buy separately.

days.note: A relaxed second day — pair a morning fort with an afternoon bazaar crawl and an optional cultural evening.

activities.name: Nahargarh Fort — city and sunset views
Johari & Bapu Bazaar — bandhani, juttis, gemstones

days.note: Only if you have a spare day and a car — Abhaneri's Chand Baori is ~95km/2h out, on the way toward Agra.

activities.name: Bhangarh Fort (the 'haunted' ruins)
activities.tip: Closes at sunset — entry is prohibited after dark.

Pushkar.name: Pushkar
Pushkar.note: A compact holy town — half a day covers the sacred core; stay a night for the sunset aarti and café culture.

days.theme: Sacred lake, Brahma Temple & sunset ghats
days.note: Everything sits around the small lake and is walkable; go barefoot near the ghats and dress modestly.

activities.name: Pushkar Lake ghats & evening aarti
activities.tip: Beware 'Pushkar Passport' flower-offering scams at the ghats — agree on nothing.

activities.name: Brahma Temple (one of the world's few)
activities.tip: No photography inside; leather items aren't allowed near the temple.

activities.name: Savitri Mata Temple by ropeway — hilltop views
Pushkar bazaar — rose products, boho cafés

meals: Sixth Sense at Inn Seventh Heaven

Jodhpur.name: Jodhpur
Jodhpur.note: One full day covers Mehrangarh and the Blue City; add a second for Umaid Bhawan and the desert-edge parks.

days.note: The fort, Jaswant Thada and the old city are close together — comfortably a single day on foot and short autos.

activities.name: Mehrangarh Fort & museum
activities.tip: Enter early; the zip-line over the ramparts is a fun optional add-on.

activities.name: Clock Tower & Sardar Market — mirchi vada, makhaniya lassi
Rao Jodha Desert Rock Park walk

Jaisalmer.name: Jaisalmer
Jaisalmer.note: Two nights is the sweet spot — one for the fort and havelis, one for the Sam dunes and an overnight desert camp.

days.note: The fort is a living town you can wander freely; the palace museum and havelis are the ticketed highlights.

activities.name: Jaisalmer Fort & Raj Mahal palace museum
Patwon Ki Haveli — carved sandstone mansions

days.theme: Sam Sand Dunes camel safari & desert camp
days.note: Sam is ~40km/1h from town — head out by mid-afternoon for a sunset camel ride and an overnight camp with folk music and dinner.

activities.name: Sam Sand Dunes camel safari at sunset
Overnight desert camp — folk dance & dinner
activities.tip: Book ahead for Oct–Mar peak season; confirm veg/vegan meals in advance.

meals: Dal baati churma cooked on site

days.theme: Havelis, war memorial & Desert National Park

Udaipur.name: Udaipur
Udaipur.note: Two days covers the lake, palace and gardens; add a third for the Kumbhalgarh and Ranakpur day trip.

days.theme: City of Lakes — palace & Pichola sunset cruise
days.note: The old city, City Palace and ghats hug Lake Pichola and are walkable — save the boat ride for the golden sunset hour.

activities.name: Lake Pichola boat ride to Jag Mandir
activities.tip: Book the sunset slot early in peak season — it sells out.

activities.name: Bagore Ki Haveli Dharohar folk dance (evening)
Sajjangarh Monsoon Palace — sunset city & lake views
Fateh Sagar Lake promenade & Nehru Garden

days.theme: Day trip — Kumbhalgarh fort & Ranakpur temples
days.note: Both are ~2h from Udaipur on the same road — leave early and combine them in one full day.

activities.name: Kumbhalgarh Fort — the 'Great Wall of India' (UNESCO Hill Fort)
Ranakpur Jain Temple — 1,444 carved marble pillars
activities.tip: Open to tourists roughly 12pm–5pm; no leather allowed inside.

connections.method: Long drive (break at Jodhpur/Ranakpur) or train/flight

extras: Jaipur's ₹550 composite ticket (valid 2 days) covers Amber Fort, Hawa Mahal, Jantar Mantar, Nahargarh, Albert Hall and more — worth it if you'll see 3+ monuments; City Palace is sold separately.
Book Jaisalmer desert camps and Sam dune safaris in advance for the Oct–Mar peak season.
Quoted fees are for Indian nationals as of Jul 2026; foreign-national fees are higher and noted where relevant — always reconfirm on-site.
Summers (Apr–Jun) hit 45°C+ in the desert — schedule sightseeing for early morning and after 4pm, and carry water.
